meaning: n.鍋囧畾,鎯宠薄,鎺ㄦ祴,鎺ㄦ兂
examples: The report is based entirely on supposition.
There's a popular supposition that we're publicly funded but the bulk of our money comes from competitive contracts.
That supposition needs to get tossed with ad rates on a one-way trip to the basement.
The problem with this supposition is the same as it was in the 1970s.
